inputs.research_topic: Quantum Magnetometer Sensors for Medical Diagnostics
inputs.keywords: quantum sensors, magnetoencephalography (MEG), magnetocardiography (MCG), SQUID replacement, optically pumped magnetometers (OPM), diamond NV centers, medical diagnostics, neuroscience research, cardiology applications
inputs.additional_information: Technology context: Startup developing room-temperature quantum magnetometers using diamond NV centers as alternative to expensive SQUID-based MEG systems. Technology advantages: No cryogenic cooling required, portable form factor, 10x lower cost than SQUID, wearable configurations possible. Market constraints: Regulatory approval required (FDA Class II medical device), integration with existing clinical workflows, competition from established MEG manufacturers, physician training requirements. Target markets: (1) Neuroscience research labs (epilepsy mapping, brain-computer interfaces), (2) Clinical neurology (pre-surgical planning, neurological disorders), (3) Cardiology applications (fetal MCG, arrhythmia detection). Analyze: TAM/SAM/SOM for each market segment, 3-5 year adoption roadmap, pricing strategy vs SQUID systems, reimbursement landscape, regulatory pathway timeline, key customer segments with decision factors, competitive positioning vs Elekta, CTF, FieldLine, and emerging quantum sensor companies. Develop go-to-market strategy prioritizing entry point and expansion path.
